response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals, excellent communication skills, and testing practices, he lacks the mandatory technical requirements for this role. His C++ experience ended in 2015 (11 years ago) with minimal hands-on work at National Instruments, falling far short of the required 5-10 years. There is no evidence of Windows platform development, and his recent career (Meta, Google) focuses on full-stack TypeScript/Node.js development, not systems or capital markets work. He has no demonstrated fixed-income derivatives or investment-banking domain expertise. A mid-senior capital markets C++ role requires both deep, recent C++ expertise and domain familiarity—neither of which are present.
strengths: Senior-level engineer with proven ability to mentor, architect, and deliver across multiple tech stacks and problem domains
Strong software craftsmanship and testing practices that align with code quality expectations in financial systems
Demonstrated SQL and database knowledge, plus experience optimizing performance-critical systems (micro-services at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second)
concerns: C++ experience ended in 2014-2015; only ~1 year of hands-on C++ work at National Instruments, far below the 5-10 year requirement
No Windows platform development experience; recent career entirely on Linux, web, and JavaScript/TypeScript stacks unrelated to capital markets
Zero demonstrated expertise in fixed-income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets—domain experience or paired expertise is mandatory

key_factors.factor: No capital markets or fixed-income domain background
Strong software engineering fundamentals and mentorship
